summary: Financial incentives have not been evaluated for cost-effectiveness in supporting smoking cessation among pregnant women; current guidelines prioritize proven behavioral strategies over monetary rewards based on limited available research. The lack of specific economic evaluations makes it impossible to determine if such programs are financially viable compared to standard care.
